assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions slices going offline 'simultaneously or in sequence' but doesn't clearly explain the atomic recreation process or distinguish it from rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'Your entire multislice job will be terminated when maintenance begins' and 'All 4 slices (1024 chips total) will go offline'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states that training will halt completely and mentions that multislice jobs require all slices to be available

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response addresses this in the recovery section mentioning 'Resource availability: 30 minutes - 2 hours post-maintenance' and discusses checking resource availability

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends 'Force a Manual Checkpoint' and provides code examples for triggering immediate checkpoints

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes detailed code examples for checkpoint managers, automatic resume capabilities, and graceful shutdown handlers

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or deferral options at all

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response mentions maintenance windows only in terms of duration ('can last several hours') but not as a way to control timing or schedule during planned breaks
